summary: Growth Activation delivery hotfix after the first Production activation was contained: corrected gtag command delivery requires fresh exact-head review and SHIP approval before any Production reactivation or DebugView claim.

planApproval.reference: START PARALLEL EXECUTION: Track A STRICT GA4 delivery correction

evidence.reference: Existing non-modal consent accessibility contracts remain in the passing 63-test Chromium suite; owner REVIEW remains pending.
Page-view readiness, successful-send deduplication and existing analytics contracts passed in the 307-test local suite.
git diff --check passed and scope inspection found no utility, registry, sitemap, crawler, brand or dependency change.
Consent remains explicit, Production-only and provider-free in Local and Preview; withdrawal contracts remain unchanged.

Owner accepts the qualified review and final activation wording for Portugal and the EU/EEA. privacy@youtoola.com is operational and monitored. Search Console, Bing, sitemap and GA4 account, property, stream and governance evidence are recorded; custom dimensions, key-event configuration, DebugView, Vercel variables and Production activation remain pending.
Growth activation, foundation, monitoring and dashboard contract tests passed locally.
The protected design-system review route and unavailable Production-mode behavior remain covered by passing tests.
npm audit reported zero vulnerabilities; no dependency changed.
package-lock.json and dependency declarations are unchanged.
Local E2E and protected Preview inspection showed no Measurement ID or Google provider request.
Production-only analytics configuration and Local/Preview rejection contracts remain validated.

Activation validation rejects unknown fields, unsupported state transitions, readiness without Measurement ID evidence, dormant DebugView success, unsupported custom-dimension or key-event success, unfounded indexing claims and accepted sitemap claims without provider evidence.

The hotfix adds a transport-shaped regression test that rejects Array commands and requires one sanitized page_view after provider readiness. Local validation passed with 309 unit tests and 63 Chromium E2E tests; Quality, End-to-end and Vercel passed against implementation head 3a1a76359b221c1ce0c79394cb23746a07dc434f.
START PARALLEL EXECUTION: Track A STRICT GA4 delivery correction
Existing bounded analytics payload contracts remain green; Unit 3 creates no utility event.

Draft PR #21 deployment dpl_2ZipcxrSRS5efhK7nZy5XWaezcBL matched implementation head 3a1a76359b221c1ce0c79394cb23746a07dc434f, was Ready and authentication-protected, returned X-Robots-Tag noindex, and all Vercel environments remained provider-variable-free.

Owner accepts qualified review YT-PRIV-2026-07-15-01 / envelope PT99T0-1300-BG dated 2026-07-15 for Portugal and the EU/EEA. The accepted policy requires future-provider blocking plus _ga and _ga_* cookie expiry on withdrawal; no privileged reasoning or personal reviewer detail is stored.
Existing analytics plain-object and unknown-field validation remains in the passing suite.

The first consented Production test loaded gtag.js but delivered no collection request because the bootstrap shim queued Arrays instead of the Arguments objects consumed by gtag.js. The hotfix changes only that queue shape and adds focused transport regression coverage; reactivation requires fresh exact-head review and SHIP approval.
No utility, registry, public route, sitemap, crawler, brand or monetisation boundary changed.
Existing redirect and canonical allowlist tests remain green.

PR #19 merged as f2434d0e6e063df40a56b6219a67981c24d774ed, but the consented page-view delivery test failed. Both Production analytics variables were removed and provider-free deployment dpl_Ak9nEPGikZMZ7i25swdJpQt6gPRB was restored. Draft PR #21 is the hotfix candidate and contains no Production-completion or DebugView claim.

Containment completed by removing both Production analytics variables and restoring provider-free deployment dpl_Ak9nEPGikZMZ7i25swdJpQt6gPRB. Keep Production dormant until this hotfix receives fresh exact-head review and SHIP approval.
Sanitized page views retain canonical allowlisted URLs, authored titles and no query, fragment or referrer.

A SHA-256 fingerprint of the owner-supplied GA4 Measurement ID is recorded as factual evidence. Activation records contain no exact Measurement ID, login email, credential, token, recovery material or DNS verification value; the approved non-secret property and stream identifiers are recorded.
Repository secret scan passed across 272 tracked files after the GA4 evidence update.

Preview retains report-only provider-free CSP and noindex headers. Live Production /privacy returned HTTP 200, HTML UTF-8, report-only CSP without Google origins, no Set-Cookie, required security headers and Vercel cache HIT.
BUILD review found no new provider, credential, external request, persistence or activation path.
Existing sensitive analytics field rejection remains green and Unit 3 creates no synthetic utility event.

Owner-verified evidence records Search Console Domain property youtoola.com via DNS TXT and Bing youtoola.com via Search Console import. Both are verified; both successfully processed https://www.youtoola.com/sitemap.xml and discovered five URLs. All five public URLs passed live inspection and are technically indexable; indexing was requested where needed, while actual indexed status remains pending.
Successful-send page-view state is ephemeral; no buffer, retry, replay or storage was introduced.

rollbackPlan.method: Keep both Production analytics variables absent and retain the restored provider-free deployment until the gtag command-delivery hotfix passes fresh exact-head review and SHIP approval.

evidence: START PARALLEL EXECUTION: Track A STRICT GA4 delivery correction
Narrow gtag command-queue correction and focused provider-transport regression test
